description: Two wrongs don't make a right, but two nots make a yes.

guides.text: The goal is $\lnot \lnot A \to A$. In everyday reasoning: if it's not the case that $A$ is false, then $A$ must be true. This is **[double-negation|double negation] elimination** — a hallmark of classical logic. (In intuitionistic logic, you don't get this for free, but Plato is classical.)
Assume the doubly-negated $A$. The [s-expression|s-expression] `(not (not A))` is parsed as $\lnot \lnot A$.
The rule `dne` ([double-negation|double negation] elimination) strips two layers of [negation|negation]. From $\lnot \lnot A$ at step 1, it gives you plain $A$. One step — that's it.
Discharge the [assumption|assumption]. The [double-negation|double negation] is gone, leaving a clean [implication|implication].

unlocks.name: ¬¬-elim
unlocks.description: From $\lnot \lnot p$ derive $p$. Eliminate a double negation — classical logic's escape hatch.
